title: Cape Reinga Tour Review
meta_desc: Honest review of Cape Reinga and Ninety Mile Beach Tour, a full-day family trip in Northland, NZ, including prices, drive times, and age suitability, with insider tips and booking advice to save money.
intro: The Cape Reinga and Ninety Mile Beach Tour is an iconic New Zealand family day trip that offers a mix of adventure, stunning scenery, and unique experiences. With prices starting at $99 for adults and $70 for children, it's essential to know what to expect. In this review, we'll cover the ins and outs of the tour, including its suitability for families with kids.

sections.heading: What's it Like with Kids?
sections.body:
The Cape Reinga tour is an excellent choice for families with kids aged 5 and above, as it offers a great balance of activity and sightseeing. The tour starts with a drive along Ninety Mile Beach, which is an exhilarating experience, especially for kids who love the thrill of riding on the beach.

The stop at the Te Paki sand dunes is another highlight, where kids can try their hand at sandboarding. However, be prepared for some physical activity, as the walk to the top of the dunes can be challenging for younger kids.

The Cape Reinga lighthouse walk is also a must-do, with stunning views of the Tasman Sea meeting the Pacific Ocean. However, the walk can be steep and rocky in some areas, so be sure to bring suitable footwear and snacks to keep the kids energized.

sections.heading: Value for Money and Insider Tips
sections.body:
The Cape Reinga tour is priced at $99 for adults and $70 for children, which is reasonable considering the duration of the tour (9-10 hours) and the unique experiences offered.

To make the most of the tour, be sure to book in advance, especially during peak season (December to February). It's also a good idea to pack plenty of water, snacks, and sunscreen, as the tour can be physically demanding.

For families, consider booking a private tour or opting for a tour operator that offers child-friendly activities and commentary.

sections.heading: Best Time to Visit and How to Book
sections.body:
The best time to visit Cape Reinga is during the summer months (December to February), when the weather is warm and sunny. However, this is also the peak tourist season, so be prepared for larger crowds.

To book the Cape Reinga tour, visit the tour operator's website or book through a local travel agent. Be sure to read the terms and conditions carefully, especially regarding cancellations and refunds.

For families, consider booking a package deal that includes accommodation and other activities, which can often work out cheaper than booking individual tours.
list: Book in advance, especially during peak season
Pack plenty of water, snacks, and sunscreen

sections.heading: Downsides for Families
sections.body:
While the Cape Reinga tour is an excellent choice for families, there are some downsides to consider. The tour can be physically demanding, especially for younger kids, and the walk to the Cape Reinga lighthouse can be steep and rocky in some areas.

Additionally, the tour may not be suitable for families with very young children (under 5), as the activities and sights may not be engaging enough for this age group.

However, overall, the Cape Reinga tour is a must-do for families who love adventure, stunning scenery, and unique experiences.
